evidence_binding_contract.source_ids_mean: Catalog-level context only; a listed source does not directly support every generated stage, signal, inference, control, or limitation edge.
evidence_binding_contract.direct_support_requires: A dedicated source edge with binding_type and the source registry's exact locator.
evidence_binding_contract.generated_relations_are: Candidate model relations derived from the authored registry, not observed provider behavior.

patterns.name: Transparent onboarding and practice interview
patterns.objective: Model how disclosed onboarding can reveal matter taxonomies, roles, authority, risk posture, and declared standard, fallback, and prohibited positions.

consent_and_authority.basis: Specific organizational authority and transparent disclosure of purpose, fields, retention, and downstream use.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Equally prominent decline with no loss unrelated to the requested service.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Firm data owner and legal or professional-responsibility review.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Abstract policy can expose crown-jewel operating logic; examples can become representation-related or work product.
what_it_cannot_prove: Correctness or universality of the declared policy
applies_when: A firm voluntarily configures a tool using abstract policy with specific authority.
does_not_apply_when: The interview is concealed, bundled, or requests real-matter strategy without specific authority.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: A transparent configuration workflow can become deceptive knowledge extraction if purpose, reuse, and refusal consequences are obscured.

patterns.name: Playbook, template, and policy import
patterns.objective: Represent how formal playbooks and templates expose normative rules, fallback ladders, escalation thresholds, drafting standards, and institutional vocabulary.

consent_and_authority.basis: Named owner approval for each source, field, processing purpose, model boundary, and reuse rule.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Local-only or no-import path must remain available.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Knowledge-management owner plus matter authority when source material derives from matters.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Playbooks may contain firm strategy, client-derived rules, work product, negotiation floors, or confidential examples.
what_it_cannot_prove: Whether formal rules match actual behavior
Whether a rule is firm-authored or client-required
Any provider's intent to generalize the material
countercontrols: Preserve source genealogy and reuse authority
Prohibit raw-source retention outside the firm
applies_when: A named owner authorizes a bounded source and the system enforces its reuse limits.
does_not_apply_when: Source provenance, client authority, privilege status, or downstream rights are unknown.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Treating all playbooks as firm-owned and reusable can leak client-derived content and erase the authority of the source matter.

patterns.name: Prompt and output processing trace
patterns.objective: Map the defensive exposure created when user requests, supplied context, model outputs, and task labels are processed to provide an AI service.

consent_and_authority.basis: Matter-specific tool authority and fact-specific review of processing, retention, access, and use.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Approved non-AI or local-only path without hidden transcript submission.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Responsible lawyer and client-specific authority where required.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Real prompts and outputs frequently contain information relating to a representation, work product, privileged communications, or personal data.
what_it_can_infer: Issue classes and desired work products
Context-choice patterns when semantic content is present
what_it_cannot_prove: Model training, retention, or cross-customer use
applies_when: The exact service and deployment necessarily process a disclosed bounded prompt to perform the requested task.
does_not_apply_when: The analysis assumes every product tier, feature, feedback path, or provider has the same data handling.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Conflating inference-time processing with retention or training creates false accusations; normalizing transcript capture creates confidentiality risk.

patterns.name: Token, latency, cost, and recurrence metadata
patterns.objective: Model how input, output, cache-read, cache-write, latency, cost, recurrence, and workflow identifiers can expose workload shape and dependency without document text.

consent_and_authority.basis: Organization-approved observability purpose with data minimization, role separation, retention, and aggregation rules.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Product analytics opt-out where operational security and billing do not require the field.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Firm security, privacy, finance, and data-governance owners.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Sparse metadata can become client- or matter-related when linked to identity, time, tool, stage, court, or outcome.
what_it_cannot_prove: Meaning or correctness of the work
That metadata is used for model training or product discovery
applies_when: Metadata fields and joins are exactly documented for a specific deployment.
does_not_apply_when: Only raw token counts are known and there is no lawful stage, identity, or semantic linkage.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Token counts can be overclaimed as semantic evidence or joined into covert personnel and matter surveillance.

patterns.name: Accept, reject, regenerate, edit, and override feedback
patterns.objective: Model how user actions around proposed outputs can reveal trust thresholds, automation boundaries, recurring exceptions, and classes of resisted recommendations.

consent_and_authority.basis: Decision events must be purpose-limited, and content-bearing feedback requires separate granular authority.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Feedback decline must not silently attach transcript, files, or surrounding context.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Responsible workflow owner and privacy review for longitudinal or personnel-linked analysis.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: When joined to the proposed content, feedback can reveal substantive strategy, individual preferences, and client instructions.
what_it_cannot_prove: Reason for the decision without authorized context
A stable attorney or firm preference
applies_when: A firm intentionally observes synthetic or authorized decision events for its own quality control.
does_not_apply_when: Decision events are silently repurposed for personnel scoring, training, or cross-customer normalization.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: A simple quality signal can become a high-value judgment trace or a misleading attorney-performance score.

patterns.name: Redline and draft-delta genealogy
patterns.objective: Represent how successive edits may expose preferred language, fallback boundaries, issue priority, drafting style, and reviewer hierarchy.

consent_and_authority.basis: Matter authority, client instructions, source genealogy, and explicit limitation to the requested representation.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Firm-local comparison without provider retention or export.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Responsible lawyer and knowledge owner before any abstraction or reuse.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Draft deltas can expose work product, negotiation posture, rejected strategies, client objectives, and tacit professional judgment.
what_it_cannot_prove: Why an edit occurred without context
applies_when: Only conspicuously fictional drafts are used in this repository's deterministic tests.
does_not_apply_when: Real or pseudonymized matter drafts, comments, or version histories are supplied.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Redline mining is one of the shortest paths from ordinary product use to a reusable negotiation and drafting ontology.

patterns.name: Tool-call, handoff, and workflow-sequence topology
patterns.objective: Model how tool names, ordered actions, handoffs, approvals, and failures can reveal systems of record and operational dependencies without parameters or results.

consent_and_authority.basis: Firm-owned observability with documented event fields, purpose, access, retention, and prohibitions on content and identity joins.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Disable nonessential workflow analytics while retaining only approved security evidence.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Tool order and timing can reveal a matter, adverse party, event, deadline, client system, or sensitive internal dependency.
what_it_cannot_prove: The content of tool parameters or results
That observed topology is firm-designed rather than vendor-default
applies_when: The event contract excludes content and is validated against an approved synthetic fixture.
does_not_apply_when: Parameters, results, paths, client identities, or real user histories are included.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Content-free traces can still become a detailed operating map and can re-identify sensitive work through joins.

patterns.name: Escalation, exception, and approval mapping
patterns.objective: Represent how escalation events and exception decisions can reveal authority graphs, severity thresholds, risk appetite, and partner bottlenecks.

consent_and_authority.basis: Role-level firm authority; rationale and identity require separate matter and personnel authority.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Local workflow execution without export of identities or rationales.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Practice leader, privacy owner, and responsible lawyer for content-bearing records.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Escalation rationale can expose legal risk, settlement posture, client priorities, and individual decision authority.
what_it_cannot_prove: Why a named decision was made
applies_when: Synthetic role and threshold events are analyzed to test defensive access controls.
does_not_apply_when: Real rationales, conflicts, negotiation floors, or individual performance data are included.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Approval telemetry can become a map of crown-jewel authority and a vehicle for covert personnel evaluation.

patterns.name: Exception rationale, debrief, and outcome loop
patterns.objective: Model the highest-value context-choice-outcome chain: stated rule, deviation, authorized rationale, decision, observed result, and proposed policy update.

consent_and_authority.basis: Explicit matter authority, client reuse rules, and a separate firm knowledge-governance decision.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Debrief remains local and unexported; no penalty for declining secondary abstraction.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Responsible lawyer, client authority where required, and firm knowledge owner.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Rationales and outcomes can expose core attorney thought process, client objectives, commercial leverage, work product, and confidential results.
what_it_cannot_prove: Provider retention, aggregation, or hostile intent
applies_when: Only fully fictional context-choice-outcome fixtures are used to validate graph semantics.
does_not_apply_when: Real settlement logic, internal advice, client objectives, or debrief narratives would be exposed.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: This loop can convert sparse matter histories into a reusable professional-judgment ontology and amplify confounded outcomes into false rules.

patterns.name: Cross-matter normalization and effective-playbook synthesis
patterns.objective: Represent the risk that recurring rules, deltas, exceptions, outcomes, and evaluation criteria are normalized across matters into a reusable policy layer.

consent_and_authority.basis: Separate authority for every source matter, client, purpose, transformation, retrieval scope, and downstream use.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Matter-local operation with no cross-matter memory, evaluation, retrieval, or model update.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Conflict, privacy, knowledge, and responsible-lawyer review.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Normalization can create cross-client contamination, reveal protected patterns, and preserve re-identifiable matter signatures despite removed text.
what_it_cannot_prove: That similar behavior is proprietary rather than client-, regulator-, court-, or vendor-driven
applies_when: Distinct synthetic matters are used solely to test that unauthorized joins are rejected.
does_not_apply_when: Real matters or pseudonymized identifiers could cross a client, matter, or ethical wall.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: De-identification can be mistaken for legal authority and can hide a cross-client knowledge transfer rather than prevent it.

patterns.name: Public docket, opinion, and deal-document reconstruction
patterns.objective: Model bounded reconstruction of public counsel, issue, judge, filing, argument, authority, timing, disposition, and transaction patterns from lawfully public sources.

consent_and_authority.basis: Lawful public access does not eliminate provenance, privacy, profiling, fairness, or terms-of-access obligations.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: No individual or firm profiling when the research purpose, source basis, or review authority is absent.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Research ethics and legal review before person- or firm-level publication.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Public sources omit sealed, restricted, unfiled, confidential, and internal material and can still enable harmful profiling or misattribution.
what_it_can_infer: Public issue, authority, counsel, judge, and outcome graph
what_it_cannot_prove: Confidential advice, discarded alternatives, settlement logic, or client objectives
Why a public position was selected
applies_when: The material is lawfully public, source-bound, unsealed, and used with explicit incompleteness warnings.
does_not_apply_when: The material is sealed, restricted, leaked, access-controlled, paywall-bypassed, or treated as complete internal strategy.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Public advocacy outputs can be mislabeled as the firm's complete private ontology and can overfit to visible winners, courts, and filed disputes.

patterns.name: Evaluation, benchmark, and challenge-set capture
patterns.objective: Represent how definitions of good work, unacceptable failure, review rubrics, and challenge cases encode professional judgment and automation boundaries.

consent_and_authority.basis: Firm ownership and source-specific authority for every case, rubric, expected answer, and reuse purpose.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Firm-local evaluation harness with replaceable models and no provider reuse.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Practice, quality, privacy, and knowledge-management owners.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Evaluation artifacts can reveal priorities, unacceptable outcomes, client-derived examples, and reviewer judgment even after text is minimized.
what_it_cannot_prove: Authority to train or improve a provider model
applies_when: Fixtures are conspicuously fictional and contain no unique real-matter fact pattern.
does_not_apply_when: Expected answers or failure cases derive from real matters without explicit source and client authority.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: An evaluation set can be more revealing than a document corpus because it states what the firm considers correct, dangerous, or valuable.

patterns.name: Support, feedback, bug-report, and shared-conversation channel
patterns.objective: Model how optional support and feedback flows can expose rare failures, edge cases, dissatisfaction, transcripts, attachments, and surrounding context.

consent_and_authority.basis: Separate, granular, revocable choice identifying exactly what transcript, files, tool calls, and context will be sent and retained.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Equally prominent rating-only or no-submission path.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Responsible lawyer and firm support-data policy.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Rare edge cases are highly informative and can accidentally include an entire matter context, client identity, files, or privileged analysis.
what_it_cannot_prove: Hostile intent from the existence of an optional feedback channel
applies_when: A dated provider policy documents the exact optional channel and the analysis stays at policy level.
does_not_apply_when: A rating silently includes content or real support material is copied into this repository.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Bundled feedback can become an accidental high-signal data transfer, while its mere existence can be misrepresented as proof of generalized training.

patterns.name: Billing, time, and matter-economics traces
patterns.objective: Represent how task codes, time, write-downs, budget variance, invoice rejections, collections, and realization expose process intensity and economic judgment.

consent_and_authority.basis: Purpose-limited finance and workflow authority with client-rule enforcement and exclusion of narratives, credentials, and payment instruments.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Local accounting and approved aggregate reporting only.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Firm finance, privacy, responsible-lawyer, and trust-accounting owners.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Time narratives and sparse economic metadata can reveal matter existence, strategy, staffing, client sensitivity, value judgment, and financial condition.
what_it_cannot_prove: Legal complexity, quality, or value from time alone
countercontrols: Keep trust and payment rails outside the model boundary
applies_when: Only synthetic financial events are used to test whether sensitive joins and routes are blocked.
does_not_apply_when: Real invoices, payment details, trust records, client narratives, or identifiable personnel metrics are present.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Operational economics can expose where a firm creates value and where it is vulnerable while also creating severe client and financial risk.

patterns.name: Co-development, managed service, and integration partnership
patterns.objective: Model how joint implementation can expose taxonomies, integration maps, evaluation methods, roadmap priorities, exception handling, and system-of-record choices without raw client files.

consent_and_authority.basis: A joint data-governance schedule defining deliverables, access, retention, derived rights, audit, exit, and prohibited purposes.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Firm-controlled implementation or replaceable bounded supplier role.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Executive sponsor, general counsel, privacy, security, knowledge, and practice leadership.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Purpose can drift from implementation help to product discovery, and operational access can reveal authority, exceptions, and institutional dependency.
applies_when: A specific public agreement or contract is reviewed with its dated access and use boundaries.
does_not_apply_when: Partnership branding is treated as proof of architecture, access, ownership, or intent.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Co-development can create deep control-plane dependency or be sensationalized as capture without evidence of actual rights and data flows.

patterns.name: Vertical integration, investment, or acquisition governance scenario
patterns.objective: Model the regulated scenario in which a technology provider seeks deeper access to operating processes, talent, evaluation culture, or economics through ownership or control.

consent_and_authority.basis: Jurisdiction-specific ownership, professional-independence, confidentiality, conflicts, client rights, fiduciary, privacy, and regulatory analysis.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: No transaction or access inference is authorized by this conceptual model.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Jurisdiction-specific legal and regulator review plus client and professional-responsibility controls.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Operating access would encounter client and professional obligations; ownership does not dissolve confidentiality, privilege, conflicts, or independence duties.
what_it_can_infer: Potential governance and operating access pathways
Required walls, rights, and review gates
what_it_cannot_prove: That a provider plans an acquisition
That any ownership form is lawful in a jurisdiction
Privilege waiver, client consent, or authority to reuse information
applies_when: Researchers compare public ownership regimes and controls without proposing evasion or a live transaction.
does_not_apply_when: The scenario is used to bypass professional independence, unauthorized-practice, privilege, confidentiality, or conflicts rules.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: A governance thought experiment can become regulatory-evasion advice or falsely imply that buying an entity buys unrestricted client knowledge.

patterns.name: Talent, expertise, and supervision graph
patterns.objective: Represent how staffing, review, mentoring, escalation, authorship, and training traces can reveal expertise concentration, succession risk, and tacit quality standards.

consent_and_authority.basis: Employee privacy, labor, professional-supervision, and purpose-specific workforce-governance authority.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Aggregate practice capability without individual external profiling.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: HR, privacy, practice leadership, and professional-responsibility review.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Identity-linked workflow data can expose individual expertise, matters, reviews, errors, relationships, and promotion signals.
applies_when: Synthetic role graphs test privacy and access invariants without names or realistic identifiers.
does_not_apply_when: Real lawyers are ranked, compared, or inferred from client-matter telemetry.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: A capability map can become intrusive workforce surveillance, discriminatory scoring, or a target list for extracting scarce expertise.

patterns.name: Provider control-plane and dependency mapping
patterns.objective: Model how hosted skills, connectors, orchestration, evaluation, identity, logging, configuration, billing, and deletion dependencies can create leverage without model training.

consent_and_authority.basis: Firm architecture, procurement, security, privacy, portability, and professional-responsibility authority.
consent_and_authority.decline_path: Replaceable model and connector path with firm-owned ontology, orchestration, logs, and evaluation.
consent_and_authority.human_gate: Architecture review and owner-approved residual-risk acceptance.

patterns.privilege_and_confidentiality_risk: Deep integration can expose workflow structure and create lock-in even when commercial terms prohibit training on customer content.
what_it_cannot_prove: Cross-customer learning or intent to replace a firm
applies_when: A specific architecture and contract are mapped with observed versus unknown boundaries.
does_not_apply_when: Capability, hosted execution, or token billing is treated as proof of retention, training, or strategic intent.
patterns.danger_if_misapplied: Ignoring non-training leverage misses real sovereignty risk; treating dependency as covert theft overstates the evidence.
